title: AstraZeneca — Hematology — briefing Q4-2025
html:
What Changed in AZ Hematology -- Q4 2025

Generated: 2026-04-25 Quarter covered: October -- December 2025 Data sources: CAL KPI Digital Tracker Q4 2025, AZ FY2025 earnings (Feb 2026), Pirtobrutinib FDA approval (Dec 2025), AMPLIFY NEJM publication, ASH 2025 presentations

1. TL;DR

  1. Pirtobrutinib gained traditional FDA approval for R/R CLL/SLL post-covalent-BTKi (Dec 3, 2025) — this formalizes the third BTKi in CLL and establishes Lilly as a credible hematology player. The immediate Calquence impact is in R/R BTKi-experienced where pirtobrutinib is now the clear standard.
  2. Calquence FY2025 revenue reached ~$3.5B+ with 13% YoY growth — solid but significantly trailing Brukinsa's ~$4.1B (+49%). The growth rate gap is the headline: Brukinsa growing 4x faster than Calquence.
  3. AMPLIFY NEJM publication and ASH 2025 data crystallize the case for A+V — 3-year PFS superiority vs CIT, strong OS trend, low cardiac AEs. The data package is complete for the February 2026 FDA submission.
  4. BeOne ASH 2025 showcase: sonrotoclax posts 91% uMRD4 in CLL combo data — an extraordinary early efficacy signal, but toxicity concerns (neutropenia, infection) noted. This is the long-term existential threat to Calquence's combination strategy.
  5. Calquence digital tracker shows messaging pivot fully underway — AMPLIFY-related messages now dominate top recall. The "combination platform" narrative is replacing the "monotherapy BTKi" narrative in promotional materials.

Client Financial Signals — AZ Q4 2025 / FY 2025 Earnings Call

Calquence FY ~$2.5B (+12%). AZ total FY: ~$54B (+18%). Oncology FY: $25.3B (+16%). AZ guided FY2026 mid-to-high single-digit revenue growth.

What AZ management said on the Q4 2025 / FY 2025 call:

  1. "Calquence is a durable growth franchise" — management maintaining the steady-growth framing, but notably not positioning Calquence as a high-growth asset. The word "durable" is chosen carefully: it manages expectations for continued low-teens growth rather than signaling acceleration.
  2. Sonrotoclax (BCL-2) partnerships building CLL combination strategy — management disclosed that AZ is developing its own BCL-2 combination approaches, signaling awareness that the CLL market is transitioning from monotherapy to fixed-duration combinations. This is the strategic context for AMPLIFY: AZ needs its own BCL-2 story to compete with BeOne's sonrotoclax.
  3. AMPLIFY data expected to drive FDA submission — management confirmed AMPLIFY as the top near-term catalyst for the hematology franchise, consistent with Q3 messaging. The NEJM publication timing (Q4 2025) and anticipated FDA filing align with a mid-2026 approval target.
  4. Management acknowledged: "AMPLIFY will determine our combination narrative" — this is the most direct statement linking Calquence's future to the AMPLIFY outcome. It confirms that AZ internally views AMPLIFY approval as a binary event for the franchise trajectory.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Analysts challenged directly: "Brukinsa hit $2.8B — are you losing the BTK war?" AZ responded: "Calquence is growing in absolute terms; the market expansion benefits everyone." This is a non-answer to the core question. Analysts are flagging the growth-rate disparity, and AZ's response (deflecting to market expansion) suggests management does not have a satisfying pre-AMPLIFY counter.
  2. Analysts probed AMPLIFY timeline and competitive positioning vs V+O — AZ was careful not to over-promise on AMPLIFY share capture, noting the combination market is "evolving." This measured tone contrasts with the bullish pre-launch ATU data (51% likely to prescribe) and suggests AZ is setting conservative external expectations.

2. Data Events That Matter

Pirtobrutinib Traditional Approval in R/R CLL

  • What happened: FDA converted pirtobrutinib's accelerated approval to traditional approval for adults with R/R CLL/SLL previously treated with a covalent BTKi (Dec 3, 2025). Based on BRUIN confirmatory data.
  • What it means: Pirtobrutinib is now firmly established as the standard of care in R/R BTKi-experienced CLL. This was already the clinical reality (ATU data showed strong pirtobrutinib preference in this segment), but the traditional approval removes any remaining hesitation. For Calquence, the R/R BTKi-experienced market is effectively ceded.
  • What Vinoth should say: "The pirtobrutinib approval is expected but has a ripple effect. It validates the non-covalent BTKi mechanism, which will accelerate interest in pirtobrutinib for earlier lines. BRUIN CLL-313 data (80% risk reduction in 1L) is the next shoe to drop — if Lilly files for 1L, we have a three-way BTKi race."

AMPLIFY NEJM Publication

  • What happened: Full Phase III results published in New England Journal of Medicine. A+V demonstrated 76.5% 3-year PFS vs 66.5% CIT; AVO showed 83.1%. Superior PFS vs CIT with HR 0.65 for A+V and 0.42 for AVO.
  • What it means: The definitive data publication. AMPLIFY's NEJM imprimatur gives physicians the peer-reviewed evidence base they've been waiting for. Combined with the anticipated FDA approval, the data package is now complete.

BeOne ASH 2025: Sonrotoclax 91% uMRD4

  • What happened: BeOne presented sonrotoclax combination data at ASH 2025 showing 91% undetectable MRD (10^-4) in CLL. Additionally, BeOne announced Phase 3 CELESTIAL-TNCLL trial (zanubrutinib + sonrotoclax vs V+O in 1L CLL) and FDA Priority Review for sonrotoclax in R/R MCL.
  • What it means: The MRD data is spectacular — 91% uMRD4 is better than any BTKi+BCL2i combination has shown. However, toxicity signals (neutropenia, infection) temper enthusiasm. If these safety concerns are manageable and Phase 3 confirms the efficacy, zanubrutinib + sonrotoclax could render AMPLIFY (acalabrutinib + venetoclax) second-best.
  • What Vinoth should say: "The sonrotoclax data is the most important competitive data point of the year. 91% uMRD4 is a step-change. But the toxicity signals are real — neutropenia and infections could limit the real-world advantage. We should start tracking sonrotoclax awareness in the CLL ATU immediately."

Calquence FY2025 Revenue

  • What happened: Calquence reached ~$3.5B+ globally for FY2025, growing ~13% YoY. AZ reported Calquence maintained leading share of US 1L CLL new patient starts.
  • What it means: The "leading 1L new starts" claim is increasingly the only metric where Calquence leads Brukinsa. Total revenue, growth rate, and overall BTKi market share all favor Brukinsa. AZ is leaning hard on the 1L CLL segment where ELEVATE-TN established the franchise.

3. Commercial Pulse

Metric Q3 2025 Q4 2025 Commentary
Calquence quarterly revenue $916M ~$950M (est.) Modest sequential growth
Brukinsa quarterly revenue $1.0B ~$1.05B (est.) Revenue gap maintained
CAL CLL rep reach — All MDs 41% (Sept) 54% (Dec) Strong Q4 push, likely pre-AMPLIFY
BRU CLL rep reach — All MDs 19% (Sept) 37% (Dec) Volatile, Q4 rebound
JAY CLL rep reach — All MDs 34% (Sept) 46% (Dec) Jaypirca promotional surge post-approval
CAL SOV Declining in Q4 vs all MD segments Flag CAL SOV decreased in Q4 across all MD segments

Promotional insight: Q4 saw a significant increase in Calquence CLL rep reach (from 41% to 54%), likely pre-loading ahead of the anticipated AMPLIFY launch. However, share of voice decreased — meaning while more physicians saw Calquence reps, the competitive noise from Brukinsa and especially Jaypirca (post-approval promotional push) diluted Calquence's voice.

4. Digital Tracker Insights (Q4 2025)

Message Recall: CLL

  • CAL30 (low afib at 6 years) — highest recall among CLL messages
  • CAL1 (90% PFS risk reduction with CAL+obinutuzumab) — highest effectiveness
  • CAL26 (lower HTN and hemorrhage vs zanu) — competitive message gaining traction
  • CAL31 (similar afib risk vs zanu) — new competitive message, moderate recall

Message Recall: MCL

  • CAL17 (ECHO: 27% PFS risk reduction with CAL+BR) — top MCL message
  • Both recall and effectiveness of Calquence messages directionally lead Brukinsa messages in MCL
  • Exception: BRU1 (superior efficacy across LoT) and BRU15 (MAIC improved PFS/CR) — these two Brukinsa messages are highly impactful

Competitive Messaging

  • Brukinsa's BRU1 message ("superior PFS vs ibrutinib" from ALPINE) remains the most impactful competitive message across all brands
  • BRU15 (MAIC improved PFS and CR vs Calquence) — a direct competitive attack that is gaining effectiveness. This is the message AZ needs to counter.

Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q4 2025

Company Brand Q4 2025 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
AbbVie/J&J Imbruvica ~$650M -22% Decline accelerating; ~$2.5B FY2025 (down from $5B+ peak)
BeiGene Brukinsa ~$750M +75% FY2025 ~$2.8B; growth outpacing Calquence in every geography
Calquence ~$950M (est.) +13% FY2025 ~$3.5B+; leading US 1L CLL new starts

AbbVie/J&J — Imbruvica (~$650M, -22%)

AbbVie Q4 2025 / FY2025 earnings:

Imbruvica's decline deepened in Q4, with FY2025 revenue at ~$2.5B — down from a $5B+ peak. Key themes:

  1. "Market evolution toward more selective BTK inhibitors" — management quote: AbbVie has effectively conceded the BTKi class to 2nd-gen agents. This language on the earnings call is significant because it signals AbbVie will not invest in defending Imbruvica's CLL position. Every quarter of reduced Imbruvica promotional investment accelerates the switch opportunity.

  2. AbbVie growing Epkinly (lymphoma) as the hematology replacement: AbbVie's hematology strategy is pivoting from BTKi (Imbruvica) to bispecific antibodies (Epkinly in lymphoma). This is a complete franchise transition — AbbVie is exiting the BTKi competitive arena, not fighting to maintain position.

  3. Decline rate accelerating: -20% in Q3 to -22% in Q4: The erosion is not linear — it's compounding. If this rate continues through 2026, Imbruvica will fall below $2B annual revenue, making it a minor player in CLL. The switch patient pool from Imbruvica is peaking NOW — there will be fewer Imbruvica patients to capture each subsequent quarter.

Implication for AZ: The Imbruvica switch opportunity is at its peak. AZ's commercial execution in the next 2-3 quarters determines how much of this declining base Calquence captures vs Brukinsa. With AMPLIFY approval expected in Feb 2026, the timing is favorable — but execution must be immediate. Any delay in AMPLIFY launch activities means Brukinsa captures more of the Imbruvica exit flow.

BeiGene — Brukinsa (~$750M, +75%)

BeiGene Q4 2025 / FY2025 earnings:

Brukinsa closed FY2025 at ~$2.8B with 49% annual growth, significantly outpacing Calquence's ~$3.5B at 13% growth. Q4 specific themes:

  1. ALPINE long-term data and label expansion widening lead: BeiGene's ALPINE data in CLL continues to mature, and label expansions into Waldenstrom's macroglobulinemia (WM) and marginal zone lymphoma (MZL) add revenue streams that Calquence does not have. Each new indication broadens Brukinsa's prescriber base and strengthens BeiGene's manufacturer credibility.

  2. BeiGene investing heavily in global commercial infrastructure: The heavy commercial investment that drove BeOne manufacturer perception gains in the ATU is backed by real spending — BeiGene's SG&A grew 30%+ as they built field forces, medical affairs teams, and market access capabilities across geographies. This is not a one-quarter spike; it's a structural build.

  3. FY2025 ~$2.8B means Brukinsa is within striking distance of Calquence's ~$3.5B: At current growth rates (Brukinsa ~49%, Calquence ~13%), Brukinsa will surpass Calquence in annual revenue during 2026. The revenue crossover in Q3 2025 was a quarterly milestone; the annual revenue crossover will be a psychological and strategic turning point.

  4. ASH 2025: sonrotoclax 91% uMRD4 data is the long-term threat: BeOne's sonrotoclax combination data presented at ASH 2025 is the most significant competitive data point of the year. If zanubrutinib + sonrotoclax proves safe and effective in Phase 3 (CELESTIAL-TNCLL), it creates a Brukinsa-based fixed-duration combination that directly competes with AMPLIFY (Calquence + venetoclax). The differentiation: sonrotoclax may offer deeper MRD responses.

Implication for AZ: The earnings data confirms what the ATU has been showing — Brukinsa is winning the growth-rate war decisively. AMPLIFY's approval must generate a step-change in Calquence's growth trajectory, not just incremental improvement. The specific metric to watch: if Calquence's growth doesn't accelerate to 25%+ within 2 quarters of AMPLIFY approval, the annual revenue crossover becomes inevitable. The sonrotoclax data adds a ticking clock — AZ has a window of 12-18 months (until sonrotoclax combination approval) to establish AMPLIFY as the standard of care in fixed-duration CLL treatment.

Key Competitive Narrative: Q4 2025

The hematology earnings picture reinforces three structural dynamics:

  1. Imbruvica decline is accelerating and AbbVie is exiting the BTKi arena — the switch patient pool is peaking. This is the best competitive environment Calquence will ever face for capturing Imbruvica defectors. After 2026, the Imbruvica pool shrinks below the level that drives meaningful share gain.

  2. Brukinsa's growth rate is 4x Calquence's, and the gap is commercial, not clinical — both agents are effective 2nd-gen BTKi. The growth differential comes from BeiGene's commercial execution (ALPINE messaging, field force investment, manufacturer perception) and Calquence's promotional intensity gap. Earnings data showing BeiGene's heavy SG&A investment explains why BeOne's manufacturer perception is surging in the ATU.

  3. The next competitive era is combination-based, not monotherapy-based — AMPLIFY (Calquence + venetoclax) vs sonrotoclax combos (zanubrutinib + sonrotoclax) will define the 2026-2028 competitive battle. Whoever establishes the fixed-duration combination standard of care first wins the narrative. AZ has a timing advantage with AMPLIFY approval imminent; BeiGene has a potential efficacy advantage with sonrotoclax MRD data.

5. What Vinoth Should Be Tracking

  1. AMPLIFY FDA PDUFA date — expected February 2026. The approval is the gateway to the post-launch commercial era. Everything pre-approval is preamble.
  2. Sonrotoclax toxicity data — the 91% uMRD4 is impressive but neutropenia/infection signals need monitoring. If toxicity limits sonrotoclax, the competitive threat diminishes. Watch for updated safety data at next major congress.
  3. Pirtobrutinib 1L regulatory filing — Lilly has not yet announced a filing timeline for BRUIN CLL-313. When they do, it becomes urgent to model the three-way BTKi scenario for the ATU.
  4. CAL SOV decline — the Q4 2025 share of voice decrease is a concern heading into the AMPLIFY launch. AZ needs to increase promotional intensity, not decrease it, in the launch quarter.
  5. CLL ATU revamp execution — the Feb 2026 design discussion sets the framework for post-AMPLIFY tracking. ZoomRx needs to execute cleanly on the redesign to retain the study.
